stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Kyiv hails frontline position as ‘strongest in a year’
stories.summary: Drones minimise Russian manpower advantage on battlefield, says foreign minister; Moscow shies from Turkey push for Zelenskyy-Putin talks. What we know on day 1,520 Ukraine’s frontline position is “the strongest” it has been in a year due to superiority in drones and enhanced air defence, said Andriy Sybiha, the foreign minister. Agence France-Presse said its analysis of data from the Institute for the Study of War (ISW) showed Russian troops made almost no territorial gains across the frontline

stories.title: Thursday briefing: ​Why the US president is losing support from crucial allies
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: As political tensions rise abroad and economic pressures mount at home, ​D​onald Trump faces a shifting landscape that is testing the loyalty of his ​M​aga supporters Good morning. Starting a war of choice that is rapidly spiralling out of control, poll ratings at a second-term low, and a cost of living crisis intensifying for millions. Any conventional US president would be in big trouble. But Donald Trump is not a conventional president, and normal rules do not seem to a

stories.title: 5 things to watch out for at EU’s crisis summit
stories.summary: NICOSIA, Cyprus — It’s not often that EU leaders head to a famous party town, but that’s what’s happening Thursday when a dinner that kicks off their next summit takes place in the resort of Ayia Napa. Sadly, the prime ministers and presidents won’t (we think) be heading down to Senior Frog’s Party Bar for cocktails as they also have a day of talks on Friday on a range of subjects, from helping the bloc navigate the war in the Middle East to making progress on the b

stories.title: The wrath of the ‘Blob’: Keir Starmer triggers dangerous war with Whitehall
stories.summary: LONDON — Keir Starmer has wrecked his relationship with thousands of civil servants working at the heart of the British state and faces a campaign from some who want see him ousted. Insiders say the atmosphere in government departments is now even worse than under former Conservative premiers Boris Johnson and Liz Truss, both of whom attacked the so-called “blob” of officialdom for thwarting their plans — and lived to regret it. The erosion of trust between officials and the government’s politic

stories.title: Los Angeles school board votes to set limits on classroom screen time
stories.summary: Measure will also limit device use during passing periods, lunch and recess and block YouTube on district devices Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The Los Angeles unified school district’s board passed a resolution on Tuesday to curb students’ classroom screen time for the upcoming school year, in the latest effort nationwide to address adverse effects from excessive device use. The measure, which passed 6-0 at a Tuesday school board meeting , will se

stories.title: Czech journalists threaten to strike over plan to scrap licence fees
stories.summary: Government aims to move TV and radio funding under state control, which critics say undermines independence Journalists at the Czech Republic’s public broadcasters have said they are prepared to go on strike unless the government of the billionaire prime minister, Andrej ​Babiš, backs down on its plan to scrap licence fees and move funding under state control. In what the journalists see as a threat to their independence, the government wants to replace the current system, in which households pa

stories.title: EU set to sign off €90bn loan for Ukraine and fresh Russia sanctions – Europe live
stories.summary: Move comes after Hungary and Slovakia dropped opposition following reopening of the Druzhba oil pipeline EU leaders are set to meet in Cyprus this evening to discuss the latest on the Middle East and the next EU budget, starting in 2028. But it looks like they will have a bit of a detour – and a reason to celebrate, too – as the long-awaited €90bn loan for Ukraine and the 20th package of sanctions against Russia are on course to be unblocked after four months of delays caused by Hungary’s Viktor

stories.title: Zelenskyy says EU unblocking of €90bn loan for Ukraine is ‘the right signal’ as Hungary drops opposition – as it happened
stories.summary: European Union formal procedures expected to conclude on Thursday as Druzhba pipeline reopens During his press conference, Fico also doubles down on his criticism of the incoming Hungarian government led by Péter Magyar, in a further sign that the relations between Bratislava and Budapest could change dramatically in the next few months. Fico has been close friends with Orbán, often teaming up with him on energy issues, but it doesn’t look like this Slovak-Hungarian partnership will continue und

stories.title: Newsletter: EU leaders weigh a NATO-style common defence clause
stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan and new Russia sanctions.

stories.title: U.S. navy chief resigns amid Pentagon infighting
stories.summary: Navy Secretary John Phelan abruptly left his job on Wednesday in part because a hugely expensive new battleship he championed sparked friction with his superiors — including Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth. Phelan, who served just over a year in his post, had helped conceive of the new battleships to curry favor with President Donald Trump, according to two people familiar with the matter. The “Trump Class” battleships were a major source of frustration for Hegseth and Deputy Secretary Stephen Fe

stories.title: EU agrees to unblock €90bn loan for Ukraine after Hungary lifts veto
stories.summary: Agreement for urgently needed loan reached after Ukraine resumed pumping Russian oil to Hungary and Slovakia EU member states have reached agreement on unblocking an urgently needed €90bn (£78bn) loan for Kyiv and a new package of sanctions against Moscow after Ukraine resumed pumping Russian oil to Hungary and Slovakia, prompting Budapest to lift its veto. Cyprus, which holds the bloc’s rotating presidency, said member states’ ambassadors had agreed to launch “written procedures” for the final

stories.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
stories.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

stories.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

stories.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
stories.summary: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

stories.title: Simor András: Az MNB zsarolása miatt mondtam le az Ersténél 2024-ben
stories.summary: A volt jegybankelnök az ATV-nek elmesélt egy történetet, amiben szerepel a Magyar Nemzeti Bank, egy ablaktalan szoba és egy baljós karácsonyi üzengetés.

stories.title: Szijjártó to Telex: The very suggestion that the Russians are holding something over us is deeply offensive
stories.summary: Outgoing Hungarian Foreign Minister Péter Szijjártó gave an interview to Telex. We asked him about the reasons for Fidesz’s crushing election defeat, their fear-mongering campaigns, Russia, the oligarchs in Fidesz's orbit, and more.

stories.title: A választás után rögtön visszavonta a kiadó a polcokról a Magyar Péter lejáratására készült képregényt
stories.summary: Az Én, a kétarcút március elején dobta piacra a Nemzeti Ellenállás Mozgalom, de úgy tűnik, a választás után már nem akarták tovább értékesíteni.

stories.title: Vissza tudja venni jogállami eszközökkel az MCC-nek juttatott vagyont az új kormány
stories.summary: Orbán Balázs, az MCC kuratóriumi elnöke az intézmény jövőjével kapcsolatban jogállami keretek közti gondolkodást kért. A kétharmados többséggel a vagyonvisszaszerzés ilyen módon valósítható meg.

stories.title: Vezérkari főnök: Orbán Gáspár is részt vett a csádi misszióval foglalkozó kormányzati tervezőcsoportban
stories.summary: De hogy a miniszterelnök fia vezette volna, az Böröndi Gábor szerint „erőteljes túlzás”. Gulyás Gergely korábban azt állította, hogy Orbán Gáspárnak nincs szerepe a csádi misszió előkészítésében.

stories.title: Magyar Péter üzent a most alkudozni akaró fideszeseknek
stories.summary: Orbán Viktort idézte a leendő miniszterelnök.

stories.title: Trump embere bedobta a FIFA-nak, hogy Irán helyett Olaszország vegyen részt a nyári foci-vb-n
stories.summary: Ezzel javítaná a Meloni és Trump között megromlott viszonyt.

stories.title: Párhuzamosan végezhetik rezidensi és doktori képzésüket a leendő orvosok
stories.summary: Ezt a lehetőséget nyitja meg a gyakorlatban is a Semmelweis Egyetem és a magyar egészségügy meghatározó intézményei között létrejött együttműködés.

stories.title: A Tiborcz-féle brókerbirodalmon belül is Orbán vereségére fogadtak nagy tételben a választás előtt
stories.summary: Az egyik equiloros alap nagy összegeket fektetett hosszú lejáratú magyar államkötvényekbe, április 12-e után rögtön csilingelt is a kassza.

stories.title: Putyin és Orbán telefonbeszélgetéséből az elfogadhatatlan szervilizmus derült ki – élőben Sz. Bíró Zoltánnal
stories.summary: Milyen lesz Magyar Péter és Putyin viszonya? Mik voltak a Szijjártó-interjú tanulságai? Milyen elszámoltatást akar a magyar társadalom? A 2026 élő adásában Sz. Bíró Zoltán Oroszország-szakértő és Szabó Andrea szociológus.

stories.title: Egy megtalálhatatlan vajdasági szervezet kapott 240 milliós támogatást a választások előtt
stories.summary: Az Agenda Polgárok Egyesületéről egyetlen cikk sem szerepel a vajdasági magyar online térben, nincs honlapja és nyilvánosan elérhető e-mail címe sem.

stories.title: 66-25: Poll shows dramatic spike in Tisza's popularity since election
stories.summary: In contrast, the Fidesz camp has shrunk significantly. Such a dramatic turnaround within such a short period of time is unprecedented in Hungary, according to the head of Medián, Endre Hann.

stories.title: Nine lessons for the US from Viktor Orbán’s defeat | Kenneth Roth
stories.summary: As US elections approach, the Hungarian prime minister’s loss is a reminder that history does not march relentlessly toward autocracy Viktor Orbán’s electoral loss was a slap in the face for Donald Trump and JD Vance, who had enthusiastically endorsed Europe’s most visible autocrat but proved unable to salvage his candidacy. But Hungarian voters’ 12 April rejection of Orbán also holds important lessons for Americans who hope to resist Trump’s own autocratic tendencies. As the November midterm el

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
stories.summary: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
stories.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

stories.title: Kigyulladt egy lakókocsi az Ecseri út mellett
stories.summary: A fővárosi hivatásos tűzoltók kiérkezésekor már az autó mellett is égett az aljnövényzet, a tűzoltók azóta már körülhatárolták a tüzet.

stories.title: A volt győri fideszes polgármester embere nem emlékezett olyanra, hogy levetkőztetett volna egy embert a lehallgatástól tartva
stories.summary: Túlárazott, indokolatlan szerződések vádjával folyik per Győrben. Az egykori résztvevőknek nincsenek emlékeik arról, hogy Dézsi Csaba András volt polgármester utasításai szerint történtek-e a dolgok a győri cégeknél.

stories.title: Orbán Gáspár távozik a honvédségtől
stories.summary: Korábbi sajtóértesülések szerint a miniszterelnök fia volt felelős a csádi misszió szervezéséért, de már a választások előtt beadhatta a leszerelési kérelmét.

stories.title: Orosz viszony, jachtozás, gyűlöletkeltés és a felesége százmilliói – interjú Szijjártó Péterrel
stories.summary: Orbán Viktor nem sértegetett senkit a poloskázással, túl sokan használták ki őket, arról viszont szinte semmit nem tud, hogy feleségének hogyan lett milliárdos vagyona egy nyilvánosan elérhetetlen cégből – Szijjártó Péter több mint három órás interjút adott a Telexnek.

stories.title: Pert indít az Erste Bank a Magyar Péter lejáratását célzó képregény miatt
stories.summary: A bank szerint a márciusban megjelent Én, a kétarcú képregényben többször is név szerint említik, és teljesen alaptalan állításokkal negatív színben tüntetik fel őket.

stories.title: Szijjártó Péter a Telexnek: Már a feltételezés is sértő, hogy az oroszok fognának minket valamivel
stories.summary: Bár erről szólt a Fidesz kampánya, ő sosem beszélt a fiainak arról, hogy elvihetik őket katonáknak, ha a Tisza nyer. Ők senkit nem zártak ki a nemzetből, néhányan kihasználták őket, Mészáros Lőrinc gazdagodásáért nem vállal felelősséget, azt pedig nem tudja, hogy lett a feleségének milliárdos cége, mondta a távozó külügyminiszter a Telex élő műsorában.

stories.title: A világ vicce, hogy a fideszesek próbálják letolni magukról az MNB-ügyet, és Matolcsyékra kenni a választási buktát
stories.summary: Az MNB-s pénzherdálás a kezdetektől tipikus példája annak, ahogyan az Orbán-kormányok alatt különféle kedvezményezett körök bántak az állami vagyonnal, ráadásul az egészhez tevőlegesen is asszisztált a Fidesz. Ha Kocsis Máté és társai azzal áltatják magukat, hogy sikerülhet ezt levakarni a pártról, akkor nincsenek tisztában sem a valósággal, sem a közhangulattal.

stories.title: Sulyok Tamás köztársasági elnök is megkapta a Laposföld-díjat
stories.summary: A Szkeptikus Társaság azokat tüntette ki, akik a Magyar Érdemrend lovagkeresztjét Wieber Orsolyának, Rákay Philip feleségének ítélték meg.

stories.title: Picasso’s Guernica is the ultimate emblem of the horrors of war. It has no place in Spain's partisan squabbles | María Ramírez
stories.summary: Forty years after the 1937 masterpiece returned to Madrid from its Franco-era exile in New York, it is again embroiled in politics Every September, Spain celebrates one of the most symbolic moments of its transition to democracy. This year will mark 45 years since an Iberia commercial flight from New York landed in Madrid with its pilot announcing to the surprised passengers that they had just travelled with one of the country’s most famous exiles: Pablo Picasso’s Guernica. After more than four

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: 'If Druzhba is operational, Zelenskyy should open it,' Magyar says about pipeline
stories.summary: "He should not go down that street," incoming Hungarian PM Péter Magyar said when asked about new potential Ukrainian demands to reopen the Druzhba pipeline. Brussels ready for Hungary to lift veto if oil flows resume.

stories.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
stories.summary: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

stories.title: Pentagon announces US navy secretary is leaving ‘effective immediately’ and replaced with deputy – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Sign up for the Breaking News US email Also today, we can expect the Senate to vote on another war powers resolution, to curb the Trump administration’s war in Iran. Led by Senator Tammy Baldwin , a Wisconsin Democrat, this will be upper chamber Democrats’ fifth attempt to pass a resolution . Louisiana v Callais: A high-stakes voting rights case in which the court’s conservative majority appears poised to gut one of the most powerful provisions of the Voting Rights Act.

stories.title: Erdogan says Iran war ‘starting to weaken Europe’ – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Follow our new live blog on the Middle East crisis here If you’re just joining us, here’s the main news of the day. It is 9.30am in Tehran, 9am in Jerusalem and Beirut, and 2am in Washington DC. Donald Trump unilaterally said he is extending the ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan’s request while awaiting a “unified proposal” from Tehran , even as the US military maintains its blockade of Iranian ports. Trump made the announcement as ceasefire talks looked increasingly uncer

stories.title: Australians ‘uneasy’ about NDIS cuts amid $53bn in new defence spending, Mark Butler concedes
stories.summary: Minister tells Guardian Australia despite substantial changes to disability scheme, it will remain one of best support services ‘anywhere in the world’ Listen to the podcast: Disability minister Mark Butler on the ‘necessary’ cuts to an NDIS ‘under pressure’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Mark Butler has defended the government’s decision to trim the NDIS just days after announcing $53bn in new defence spending,

stories.title: Top Cabinet Office official to give evidence to MPs about Peter Mandelson’s vetting – UK politics live
stories.summary: Permanent secretary, Cat Little, to give evidence to foreign affairs committee as pressure increases on Keir Starmer Good morning. As Kiran Stacey , Pippa Crerar and Jessica Elgot report in the Guardian’s splash, “Keir Starmer is looking increasingly isolated over his handling of the Peter Mandelson scandal with divisions emerging in cabinet over his decision to sack the Foreign Office civil servant Olly Robbins.” And this morning the saga continues, with Cat Little , permanent secretary at the

stories.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.title: Roman Abramovich takes Jersey to European human rights court over criminal investigation
stories.summary: Lawyers for oligarch claim freezing of £5.3bn of assets ‘unfair and abusive’ amid row over use of funds for Ukraine Roman Abramovich has gone to the European court of human rights (ECHR), claiming that a criminal investigation into his financial affairs by the Jersey authorities has breached his human rights, according to reports. The former owner of Chelsea FC, who is under UK sanctions over his links to Vladimir Putin, is being investigated in Jersey over allegations of corruption and money la

stories.title: ‘Impossible’ to reopen strait of Hormuz amid ‘flagrant’ ceasefire breaches, Iran says
stories.summary: Iranian forces seize two ships in critical waterway as Washington and Tehran maintain separate blockades Middle East crisis – live updates Iranian forces have seized two ships in the strait of Hormuz as the US and Iran doubled down on imposing separate blockades of the shipping waterway. The standoff over the strait – through which about 20% of the world’s oil and liquefied fossil gas passed through during peacetime – has raised doubts about whether stalled peace negotiations will resume. stories.title: Mysterious death of Jackson Stacker near Byron Bay referred to homicide squad
stories.summary: Melbourne man stabbed twice in chest, with remains found in field near his campervan in 2021. Family argue police prematurely assumed he killed himself Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales coroner has recommended the mysterious death of Jackson Stacker near Byron Bay be investigated by the homicide squad, with Teresa O’Sullivan finding “some aspects of the early [police] investigation raised concer

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: US and Iran in blockade stalemate as Washington’s navy secretary leaves office ‘immediately’
stories.summary: Iran says two seized ships transferred to its coast; US navy secretary exiting post ‘effective immediately’, says Pentagon ‘Impossible’ to reopen strait of Hormuz amid ‘flagrant’ ceasefire breaches, Iran says The Pentagon abruptly announced that the secretary of the US navy, John Phelan, would be leaving his job yesterday. No reason was given for the unexpected departure of the navy’s top civilian official, who had addressed a large crowd of sailors and industry professionals at the navy’s annua

stories.title: Nepal: Interior minister elected on anti-corruption platform steps down amid financial investigation
stories.summary: Nepal's new government elected on an anti-corruption platform after youth protests toppled its predecessor has now lost two ministers in weeks. Sudan Gurung said he was quitting to ensure "a fair investigation."

stories.title: He wasn't guilty but delays left this man jailed for five years without trial
stories.summary: At 17, Rasheed Wasiu was detained as security forces cracked down on protests against police brutality.

stories.title: Diplomacy stalls as Iran fires on three ships in the Strait of Hormuz and US maintains blockade
stories.summary: Diplomatic talks between Iran and the United States appear to stall, with Tehran rejecting the premise of talks until Washington lifts its naval blockade of its ports. The international community has urged all parties to de-escalate and engage with diplomatic efforts to bring an end to the war.

stories.title: Kazakhstan confirms suspension of its oil transit to Germany via Russia
stories.summary: Kazakhstan’s energy minister announced Wednesday there would be no transit of its oil to Germany through Russia throughout May due to Russian technical constraints, assuming it is “related to recent strikes on Russian infrastructure".

stories.title: UN rights chief alarmed by number of drugs-related executions in Singapore
stories.summary: Officials in Singapore maintain that the death penalty has made it one of the world's safest cities and a government survey in 2023 showed strong public support for capital punishment for serious crimes.

stories.title: UK divers prepare for mine-clearing operations in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: LONDON — British military divers are preparing to conduct mine-clearing operations in case they are needed in the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Ministry of Defence. The Royal Navy experts, trained in mine neutralization and clearance, are being readied to provide additional options alongside uncrewed systems. As military planners take part in a second day of talks hosted in London, the U.K. also confirmed it will offer autonomous mine-hunters as part of a proposed multinational mission

stories.title: Europe’s auspicious renewable: Hydro power
stories.summary: Elisabeth Braw is a senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, author of the award-winning “Goodbye Globalization” and a regular columnist for POLITICO. Her new book, “ The Undersea War ,” is out later this year. Oil! Oil! Oil! Since Feb. 28, the world has been able to focus on little else. Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to the U.S. and Israel’s war has fueled an oil shortage, replete with sky-high energy prices and temporary factory closures. Such is the scope of the

stories.title: Two US officials who died after Mexico drug raid reported to be CIA agents
stories.summary: Mexico to investigate possible breach of its constitution and assess US’s role in anti-drug operation near Chihuahua Mexico has launched an investigation into a possible breach of its constitution as it was reported that two US embassy officials who died in a car accident while returning from a raid on a drug lab with local officials in the border state of Chihuahua were CIA operatives. The accident happened early on Sunday, as the officials were driving back from the scene of the raid. Their ve

stories.title: Israeli strike kills journalist after ongoing attacks blocked rescuers, Lebanon says
stories.summary: Amal Khalil had been buried in rubble after an Israeli strike that also injured another journalist, Zeinab Faraj Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed a journalist on Wednesday after rescuers were blocked from accessing the building where she was buried under rubble because of further Israeli fire, according to several witnesses. Amal Khalil was covering developments near the town of al-Tayri with the photographer Zeinab Faraj when an Israeli strike hit the vehicle in front of them. Continu

stories.title: Pentagon says navy secretary is leaving, marking another top leader’s departure
stories.summary: Exit of John Phelan, navy’s top civilian official, comes a week after Pete Hegseth fired army’s top officer The Pentagon announced on Wednesday that the navy’s top civilian official, John Phelan, the secretary of the navy, is leaving his job. In a statement posted to social media, Sean Parnell, a Pentagon spokesperson, said Phelan was “departing the administration, effective immediately”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Number of billionaires globally could reach 4,000 in next five years
stories.summary: There are now 3,110 billionaires but analysis shows ‘deep structural acceleration’ in wealth creation around world The number of billionaires in the world could reach nearly 4,000 by 2031, figures suggest, as the super-rich accumulate wealth at an accelerating rate. There are now 3,110 billionaires globally, according to analysis by the estate agent Knight Frank. This is forecast to rise by 25% over the next five years, taking the total to 3,915. Continue reading...

stories.title: Pilot’s selfie led to mid-air collision in F-15K fighter jet, says South Korea’s air force
stories.summary: One pilot ordered to repay some of the $600,000 of damage caused by collision in 2021 South Korea’s air force has apologised for a 2021 mid-air collision involving two fighter jets, a day after auditors said pilots were taking selfies and filming during the flight and held them responsible for the accident. “We sincerely apologise to the public for the concern caused by the accident that occurred in 2021,” an air force spokesperson said in a press briefing. The spokesperson said one of the pilot

stories.title: Has the world grown weary of art biennials? In search of an antidote, a Portuguese festival turns to anarchism
stories.summary: Art festivals can fill abandoned buildings with new life – or clear a path for property developers. Coimbra’s Anozero is trying out a more confrontational approach If you decide to spend a night at Coimbra’s Monastery of Santa Clara-a-Nova in the near future, do bear in mind that the place is almost certainly haunted. Disembodied children’s voices echo around the first floor of the 17th century convent perched atop a hill in the Portuguese university city, overlooking the medieval centre from ac

stories.title: Leading California governor candidates spar in debate as topsy-turvy race heats up
stories.summary: Six candidates clashed over homelessness and cost of living crisis in first debate since Eric Swalwell’s exit – with a clear frontrunner still yet to emerge Six candidates vying to become the next governor of California sparred on Wednesday in the first debate since the already topsy-turvy race was plunged into upheaval by the sudden collapse of former congressman Eric Swalwell’s campaign after sexual assault and misconduct allegations . With a clear frontrunner still yet to emerge, the unusuall

stories.title: Schools forced to cut back on support for Send pupils in England, poll finds
stories.summary: More than 70% have cut down in past year on teaching assistants, who play key role in helping children with Send Two-fifths of school leaders in England have been forced to cut back on support for children with special educational needs due to a financial crisis “more than a decade in the making”, according to a poll. Seven out of 10 (71%) leaders say they have cut down on teaching assistants (TAs) in the past year, while 49% have reduced support staff. The crisis could escalate as 81% warn of f

stories.title: Private secretary of billionaire Judith Neilson refused bail on $1m fraud charges
stories.summary: Annalouise Spence allegedly used credit card to make ‘unauthorised purchases of luxury and personal items, including clothing, artwork and jewellery’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The former private secretary of billionaire philanthropist Judith Neilson has been charged with dozens of counts of fraud after she allegedly used a business credit card to make more than $1m worth of purchases, including luxury cloth

stories.title: David Malouf, Australian author of Remembering Babylon and Ransom, dies aged 92
stories.summary: Acclaimed Brisbane-born writer was known for his work exploring his own childhood, great myths and colonial Australia David Malouf, the acclaimed Australian author of books including Ransom, An Imaginary Life and the Booker prize-nominated Remembering Babylon, has died aged 92. Malouf died on Wednesday, his publisher, Penguin Random House Australia, said in a statement on Thursday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Sainsbury’s says impact of Iran war may lead to drop in profits this year
stories.summary: Retailer WH Smith joins supermarket in warning on effects of Middle East conflict Sainsbury’s has warned that profits could fall this year as the conflict in the Middle East squeezes customers’ budgets and pushes up business costs. The supermarket group said the conflict “will impact both our customers and our business” and it was unclear how large the effect would be. Continue reading...

stories.title: Head-on train collision near Copenhagen leaves four critically injured
stories.summary: At least 17 people hurt after incident involving two local services north of Denmark’s capital Two local trains have collided head-on in Denmark, injuring at least 17 people, four of whom are in a critical condition, according to emergency services. The public broadcaster DR showed images of two yellow and grey trains, both with damage to the front, facing each other in a wooded area. Continue reading...

stories.title: City firms bank on ‘savvy squirrel’ advertising campaign to push Brits towards investing
stories.summary: The campaign is part of government initiative to boost financial risk taking, amid fears UK growth is being stymied Nils Pratley: the ‘Savvy Squirrel looks a hit too tame’ City firms are pinning their hopes on a government-endorsed advertising blitz fronted by a finance “savvy” CGI squirrel to encourage cautious British savers to shift out of cash and start investing. The long-awaited retail investment campaign, which will cost up to £50m, is part of the chancellor Rachel Reeves’ nationwide push

stories.title: UK undershoots annual borrowing target by £700m
stories.summary: But Iran war likely to blow hole in Rachel Reeves’s carefully crafted fiscal ‘headroom’ in coming months Business live – latest updates The UK government budget came in below its annual borrowing target by £700m, official figures show – but the Iran war is likely to blow a hole in Rachel Reeves’s carefully calculated fiscal “headroom” over the coming months. The government borrowed a net total of £132bn for the financial year ending in March, the Office for National Statistics (ONS) said. This s

stories.title: Australia news live: Rinehart’s $200m donation to convert homes for veterans welcomed by RSL; Minns says about a third of NDIS cuts will be in NSW
stories.summary: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast James Valentine’s family has released a statement after his death. Here’s what they had to say: James passed peacefully at home surrounded by his family, who adored him. Throughout his illness, James did it his way, which lasted all the way until the end when he made the choice to do Voluntary Assisted Dying. Both he and his family are grateful he was given the option to go out on his own terms. He was calm, di

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz is hosting gunboat diplomacy as US and Iran vie for most effective blockade
stories.summary: Iran’s goal is to maintain chokehold on the global economy, even as some say it could run out of oil storage by Sunday Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump’s indefinite shelving of the plan to bomb Iran’s bridges and power stations on Tuesday night is being widely described as leaving the conflict in limbo, but that is anything but the truth. Pakistan insists the prospect of talks in Islamabad has not evaporated, and positive messages are still being exchanged, but in the meantime the

stories.title: School's out in Iran: No phone, no internet, no classes
stories.summary: Iran's schools are closed because of the war. Classes are being held via TV programs and the national intranet. Children without a laptop or smartphone are left out. The country is effectively cut off from the internet.

stories.title: Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the public would not accept climate policies that lead to deindustrialization, warning that they would hinder progress and innovation.

stories.title: UK, France agree 3‑year deal to curb Channel crossings
stories.summary: For the first time, the deal links some UK payments to France to performance in stopping migrants. Funding will be halted after one year if targets are not met.

stories.title: Iran war: Standoff at Hormuz casts shadow over Iran ceasefire talks
stories.summary: The prospects for renewed peace talks wavered after Iran seized two international vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Palantir: Why its political manifesto is causing a stir
stories.summary: The US tech company has posted a 22-point manifesto on X that goes well beyond typical corporate communications. Critics say it is evidence of "technofascism."

stories.title: Denmark: Two trains collide injuring several
stories.summary: Two trains have crashed into each other in Denmark, leaving several people injured, according to media reports.

stories.title: India news: Tamil Nadu, West Bengal cast votes
stories.summary: Polling has begun in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal in high-stakes assembly elections. DW has more.

stories.title: Lebanon says Israel targeted journalist killed in airstrike
stories.summary: Amal Khalil was killed when a house she was sheltering in was hit by an Israeli airstrike. Lebanon has accused Israel of targeting media workers as part of an "established approach" — a charge Israel denies.

stories.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'
stories.summary: In an interview with DW, Ron Prosor, Israel's ambassador to Germany, defends his country's military strikes against Iran and Lebanon. He also advises Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz to show more "tact."

stories.title: ICC blocks ex-Philippine President Duterte release, says it can try him
stories.summary: Judges ruled the court has authority to try the former Philippine leader despite the Philippines' exit from the ICC.

stories.title: Japan: South Korean arrested outside Yasukuni Shrine ceremony for war dead
stories.summary: Police said a South Korean national holding a banner with political messages was arrested at Tokyo's Yasukuni Shrine to Japan's war dead. Japan's prime minister did not attend proceedings, but around 120 lawmakers did.

stories.title: Beijing artist marks World Earth Day with peace installation
stories.summary: Chinese artist Kong Ning marks World Earth Day in Beijing with a peace-themed installation, using wearable art to highlight environmental concerns.

stories.title: China weathered Trump's tariffs - but the Iran war is taking a toll
stories.summary: The Middle East conflict is putting pressure on factory orders, costs and jobs in China's export-driven economy.

stories.title: Zambia's government takes possession of ex-president's body in repatriation row
stories.summary: Zambia authorities and Edgar Lungu's family are in a dispute over where former leader's remains should be buried.

stories.title: Girl, 14, found in singer D4vd's car died of 'multiple injuries'
stories.summary: The medical examiner said the 14-year-old's cause of death was determined months ago but was blocked from release.

stories.title: US Navy chief leaving post 'effective immediately', Pentagon says
stories.summary: US Navy Secretary John Phelan is the latest high-ranking military leader to leave the administration in recent months.

stories.title: Pope Leo criticises Equatorial Guinea prisons as he winds up Africa tour
stories.summary: The Pope gives a frank and passionate speech, the likes of which have characterised his tour of Africa.

stories.title: Synthetic drugs and digital crime: Why Central Asia is emerging as a new frontline
stories.summary: The rise of synthetic drugs and digital trafficking methods is forcing a shift in global anti-narcotics strategies. Uzbekistan is advancing new initiatives to improve cross-border cooperation, financial oversight and public awareness.

stories.title: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots taking pictures, report finds
stories.summary: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

stories.title: Iran executes former atomic agency employee amid Mossad spy claims
stories.summary: Iran executes former atomic energy employee Mehdi Farid for alleged Mossad links as rights groups decry opaque trial, coerced confessions and media blackout.

stories.title: Russia faked economic data to appear more resilient to its war and sanctions, intel report says
stories.summary: Swedish intelligence said Russia has been covering up a weak, debt-driven economy hit by war costs and sanctions, warning of rising inflation and banking risks.

stories.title: British woman died in Ghana trying to recoup money from scammers, inquest told
stories.summary: Janet Fordham died in crash after travelling to see man who claimed he would help to recover money from earlier scams A British woman who was scammed out of up to £1m in a string of so-called romance frauds died in a road crash after travelling to west Africa to try to recoup some of her lost fortune, an inquest in Devon has heard. Janet Fordham was cheated of her life savings and her home over a period of five years by fraudsters apparently based in the UK, Germany, the US and Ghana, the inques

stories.title: Tourist charged with damaging historic Florentine fountain in pre-wedding prank
stories.summary: Police catch woman, 28, climbing colossal 16th-century statue of Neptune to touch its genitals as a dare A tourist has been charged after allegedly climbing a colossal marble statue in Florence to touch its genitals for a pre-wedding prank. Experts said the woman caused thousands of euros of damage to the Neptune fountain in Piazza della Signoria. Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran war: Jet fuel crisis deepens as Lufthansa cuts flights
stories.summary: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.

stories.title: Could Iran war trigger bigger trade crisis than COVID?
stories.summary: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts ask whether it could reshape supply chains more deeply than COVID-19, forcing companies to rethink strategies to counter further geopolitical shocks.

stories.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
stories.summary: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

stories.title: Iran war: Revolutionary Guard says seizes ships in Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran's naval forces accused two ships of endangering maritime security by attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz without authorization. The escalation comes amid uncertainty over US-Iran ceasefire talks. DW has more.

stories.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
stories.summary: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

stories.title: Russia to block Kazakh oil flows on key pipeline supplying Berlin
stories.summary: Germany has confirmed that Russia will block flows of Kazakh oil to a vital refinery that supplies much of Berlin's energy needs from May 1.

stories.title: Iran war fuels crisis for Africa's aviation sector
stories.summary: The Iran war is pushing up jet fuel prices, forcing African airlines to raise ticket prices, cancel flights and rethink operations to stay financially viable.

stories.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting
stories.summary: Highly controversial, but not well understood: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil wants to change the tax benefits for married couples.

stories.title: Pope Leo decries inequality in corrupt Equatorial Guinea
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV continued his tour of Africa with a Mass in Equatorial Guinea. The pontiff called for more justice in the country as the nation's president and his family looked on.

stories.title: EU proposes steps to deal with energy crisis amid Iran war
stories.summary: Brussels has unveiled a toolbox to manage energy shocks, as concerns grow over fuel supplies and rising costs. It includes a push to decrease the bloc's reliance on fossil fuels.

stories.title: Israelis mark Memorial Day during ongoing conflict
stories.summary: Israelis marked Memorial Day with ceremonies and informal tributes, reflecting on loss while continuing daily life amid ongoing conflict and uncertainty.

stories.title: US charges anti-extremism organisation over payments to informants in hate groups
stories.summary: The Southern Poverty Law Center said it would "vigorously defend ourselves, our staff, and our work".

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV condemns ‘lust for power’ and inequality in Equatorial Guinea
stories.summary: The Holy Father of the Catholic Church made a stop in the final leg of his four-country tour of Africa. Equatorial Guinea is a secular country, with around three-fourths of its population Catholic.

stories.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards tighten grip on power as civilian leadership sidelined
stories.summary: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard has evolved from a theocratic wartime force into a dominant military-economic power. Over decades, it expanded regionally, reshaped Iran’s internal balance of power, now standing at the centre of a shifting political structure amid leadership changes and consolidation.

stories.title: Fed chair nominee Warsh rejects 'Trump sock puppet' label at Senate hearing
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh, the nominee for Chair of the US Federal Reserve, declared he has not pledged to lower interest rates to secure his position despite public calls from US President Donald Trump for immediate monetary easing.

stories.title: Virginia approves redistricting measure, boosting Democrats' hopes for midterms
stories.summary: The new maps approved by the state's voters could allow Democrats to flip as many as four House seats.

stories.title: Los Angeles becomes first major US school district to limit classroom screen time
stories.summary: The school district, which serves about half a million students, is looking to reduce its reliance on devices after the pandemic.

stories.title: No cartels involved - but Mexico's pyramid attack prompts new concerns
stories.summary: The deadly shooting at a popular tourist site weeks before the World Cup has rocked Mexico.

stories.title: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as fuel prices surge
stories.summary: The airline is the latest to cut flights as the US-Israel war with Iran sends jet fuel prices soaring.

stories.title: Madrid hosts Experience Macao Roadshow showcasing culture, food and tourism
stories.summary: The Experience Macao Roadshow in Madrid offered visitors interactive activities, cultural performances and gastronomy highlighting the Asian territory’s tourism appeal.

stories.title: Syria's president arrives in Qatar for talks after Saudi Arabia visit
stories.summary: Syria’s president Ahmed al-Sharaa visited Doha on Wednesday for talks with Qatar’s emir on economic cooperation, investment and post-al-Assad regional reintegration.

stories.title: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.summary: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.title: Nigeria's suspected coup plotters deny treason charges
stories.summary: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

stories.title: Aerial footage shows widespread destruction in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: A 10-day ceasefire went into effect in Lebanon on Friday, but there have been several Israeli strikes and Hezbollah claimed its first attack on Tuesday.

stories.title: Billionaire backer sues Trump family's crypto firm over alleged extortion
stories.summary: Billionaire investor Justin Sun is suing the family's World Liberty crypto venture after spending $45m on its tokens.

stories.title: Second French peacekeeper dies after ambush blamed on Hezbollah, Macron says
stories.summary: Established in 1978, UNIFIL has more than 10,000 peacekeepers from 50 countries who patrol the Blue Line, the UN-drawn border between Lebanon and Israel.

stories.summary: Igaz, csak egy napjuk volt rá: az, amelyiken az Elten nevű kisvárost 1963 nyarán visszacsatolták Németországhoz.

stories.summary: A 28 éves turistát a rendőrök szedték le az emlékműről.

stories.summary: Nagyjából négy hete van felkészülni 141 politikai újoncnak a parlamenti munka kezdetéig, amihez jogi kisokost kaptak, be kell magolniuk a házszabályt és az alkotmányt, képezik magukat. Van, aki arról is kapott tanácsokat egykori képviselőktől, hogy hogyan ne tévedjen el a Parlamentben, és mit tegyen, ha nagyon fáradt lesz a sok vezetéstől.

stories.summary: Rétvári több mint 53 órán át beszélt, hárman egyszer sem szólaltak fel a 2022-2026 közötti négyéves ciklusban.

stories.summary: Harmincan megsérültek az Institute városában lévő ezüstfeldolgozóban.

stories.summary: Felmérést végeztek a lakók körében, és kis különbséggel a forgatást ellenzők kerültek többségbe.

Down in the hold, the mood had settled into the familiar rhythm of a fishing day drawing to a close. “We were just working, waiting for the last trawler to return,” said Jhonny Sebastián Palacios, one of the fishers. “Everything was perfectly fi

stories.title: Student allegedly jailed in China for six years after taking part in pro-democracy protests in Australia
stories.summary: Exclusive: The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect overseas students from political repression The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect Chinese international students from political repression by authorities on their return after a Chinese student was allegedly sentenced to six years’ imprisonment for joining pro-democracy protests in Australia. The student, who the Guardian has chosen not to name, lost contact with his frien

stories.title: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te abandons visit after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoke overflight permission Taiwan’s president, Lai Ching-te, has cancelled his trip to Eswatini, the democratic island’s only diplomatic ally in Africa, after his government said several countries had revoked overflight permits because of “intense pressure” from China. Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. Continue reading...

stories.title: Millions in India stripped of vote before critical state election, as government seeks to ‘purify’ electoral roll
stories.summary: Experts say Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from the electoral roll ahead of the West Bengal elections this week Millions of people in the Indian state of West Bengal have been stripped of their vote ahead of a critical state election this week, after a controversial electoral revision described by critics as a “bloodless political genocide” and mass disenfranchisement of minorities. In West Bengal, a total of 9.1 million names have been deleted from the registe

stories.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
stories.summary: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...

stories.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
stories.summary: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers. Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2

stories.title: What course will Bulgaria’s new leader Rumen Radev take?
stories.summary: The trained pilot, former general and ex-president says he will crack down on corruption and seek dialogue with Russia. But who is Rumen Radev, the man who swept to victory in Sunday’s election?

stories.title: Trump wants to extend Iran ceasefire, until negotiations conclude
stories.summary: The US president also wrote on Truth Social that the blockade of Iranian ports will continue until Tehran presents a "unified proposal".

stories.title: Russia and North Korea connect road bridge ahead of summer opening, Moscow says
stories.summary: Ties between both countries have surged amid Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, with Moscow and Pyongyang deepening economic, political, cultural and military links.

stories.title: Qatar backs ceasefire as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad
stories.summary: Qatar has urged diplomacy on Tuesday as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran talks, warning prolonged tensions risk wider global fallout, particularly if disruption in the Strait of Hormuz affects energy flows and markets.

stories.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.summary: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

stories.title: Israeli army jails two soldiers for smashing head of Jesus statue in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had said on Monday he was "stunned and saddened to learn that an IDF soldier damaged a Catholic religious icon in southern Lebanon."

stories.title: German police launch mass raids of 50 premises to smash Syrian trafficking ring
stories.summary: Police believe the ring operated by using genuine residency documents issued to Syrian refugees in Germany, which were sent to people in Syria with a similar appearance.

stories.title: Meta to track workers' clicks and keystrokes to train AI

stories.summary: The Conflict Insights Group (CIG) says its research also shows the extent of UAE involvement.

stories.title: Myanmar military regime widens sanitary towel ban, claiming rebels use them for first aid
stories.summary: Activists say clamp down on period products to target insurgents is gender-based violence and violates rights Myanmar’s military regime is expanding its ban on the distribution of period products, claiming they are being used to treat wounded resistance fighters, according to local activists. The south-east Asian country has been locked in civil war since 2021 , when the military usurped the democratic government and launched a violent crackdown on dissidents. stories.title: Almost 43,000 migrants register in first three days of Spain's regularisation amnesty
stories.summary: Spain's position sharply differs from prevailing attitudes on immigration in Europe, where many governments have been trying to curb arrivals and step up deportations.

stories.title: George Ariyoshi, first US governor of Asian American descent, dies aged 100
stories.summary: Democrat led Hawaii from 1973 to 1986, coinciding with the party’s rise to power in the state George R Ariyoshi – Hawaii’s former governor and the nation’s first Asian American governor – has died at age 100. Ariyoshi, a Democrat who led the state from 1973 to 1986, died peacefully while surrounded by family on Sunday night, according to a statement Monday from the current governor, Josh Green. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK agriculture deal with EU will not remove all red tape, peers told
stories.summary: Lords told sales of Scottish shellfish among areas that may benefit – but agreement will not erase all paperwork Europe live – latest updates A new agriculture agreement with the EU will not wipe out all Brexit paperwork but might pave the way for sales of Scottish langoustines and oysters, the House of Lords has heard. stories.title: Carney names broad team to advise on tense US-Canada trade talks
stories.summary: Conservatives and former provincial premiers among those PM names to advisory committee on economic relations Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney , says his new advisory committee on economic relations with the United States will draw on the “best advice and the broadest perspectives” as the country braces for what many expect will be tense trade negotiations with its southern neighbour. The 24-member advisory committee, announced on Tuesday, shows the prime minister’s eagerness to reach across

stories.title: Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghans who aided US forces to Congo
stories.summary: Discussions reportedly come after Trump’s decision to stop initiative that allowed group to apply to resettle in the US The Trump administration is in discussions to potentially send up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war in Afghanistan to the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a non-profit confirmed on Tuesday. The resettlement talks, first reported by the New York Times , come after Donald Trump’s decision to stop an initiative that allowed Afghans who assisted US war efforts

stories.title: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex
stories.summary: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said. stories.title: Külsős vizsgálat indult a Gates Alapítvány és Jeffrey Epstein közötti kapcsolat felderítésére
stories.summary: Az Epstein-iratokban több Bill Gates és Epstein közötti email is szerepel.

stories.title: 66-25: Brutálisan elhúzott a Tisza a választás óta egy friss Medián kutatás szerint
stories.summary: A Fidesz tábora pedig jelentősen megcsappant. A megkérdezettek 54 százaléka szerint jó irányba mennek a dolgok a Tisza győzelme óta, ez 21 százalékos növekedés. Hann Endre szerint ekkora fordulatra ilyen rövid idő alatt még nem volt példa.

stories.title: Nem lesz bizalmi szavazás a kajak-kenu szövetség elnöke ellen
stories.summary: Az MKKSZ szerint nincs szükség a szavazásra, amit Schmidt Gábor kért maga ellen.

stories.title: Lezsák Sándor a tiszás kétharmad miatt kéri, hogy támogassák az alapítványát
stories.summary: Az Országgyűlés fideszes alelnöke szerint veszélybe került a Lakiteleki Népfőiskola programjainak a támogatása.

stories.title: Egy férfi és fia símaszkban machetével rátört egy családra, a házat is megpróbálták felgyújtani
stories.summary: Több, engedély nélkül tartott lőfegyvert és lőszert is találtak náluk.

stories.title: Kapitány István: Kilenc százalék lesz a minimálbér után fizetendő személyi jövedelemadó
stories.summary: A leendő miniszter szerint mindenki kevesebb SZJA-t fog fizetni, aki a mediánbér alatt keres.

stories.title: A táncoló Hegedűs Zsoltról is beszélt John Oliver a Last Week Tonight műsorában
stories.summary: „Hihetetlen, hogy egy autokrata bukását úgy ünneplik, mintha a legrészegebb nagybácsikád táncolna egy esküvő utolsó 20 percében” – kommentálta a humorista Hegedűs korábbi táncát.

stories.title: Az MTA visszafogadná a HUN-REN kutatóhálózatát
stories.summary: Az Akadémia nyilatkozatot adott ki arra, hogy nyitott a kutatóintézetek visszafogadásáról. Azt írták: ha lesz megállapodás, annak meg kell szüntetnie az akadémiai ingatlanvagyon Alaptörvényt sértő használatát is

stories.title: Why cloud computing still runs on coal and gas
stories.summary: Renewable energy is increasingly being used to supply power-hungry data centers. As the sector swells, much of the electricity demand is being met by polluting fossil fuels.

stories.title: Apple switches CEO: What it means for the tech giant
stories.summary: Apple's top hardware engineer John Ternus will lead the $4 trillion iPhone maker, as Tim Cook steps back. Will Apple chase rivals for the smartest intelligence, or deliver the best personal AI experience?

stories.title: Mars: NASA's Curiosity rover finds new organic matter in Gale Crater
stories.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover has identified more organic compounds as scientists investigate any potential ancient life signs on Mars. It was exploring the massive Gale Crater also believed to be a now-dry lake.

stories.title: UK moves to ban smoking for everyone born after 2008
stories.summary: A draft law in the UK to create a "smoke-free generation" by banning smoking for anybody born after 2008 has cleared both houses of parliament. Only the king's signature remains for it to become law.

stories.title: How grievance turns to violence in a mass shooter's mind
stories.summary: Attacks like last week's school shooting in Turkey can seem to come out of nowhere, but they rarely do. Instead, a buildup of grievance and fixation often precedes the the moment violent thoughts become violent acts.

stories.title: Record number of immigrants reside in EU in 2025
stories.summary: The foreign‑born population of the EU grew to around 64 million in 2025. Germany remains the biggest host of immigrants in absolute terms, but some smaller nations have a much higher percentage of foreign-born residents.

stories.title: Timelapse footage shows maritime traffic around Gulf, Straight of Hormuz
stories.summary: The passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz - the world's busiest oil shipping canal - has been severely disrupted amid the US-Israeli war with Iran. Timelapse footage shows maritime traffic in the Gulf and Strait of Hormuz from April 18 to April 20, amid a fragile US-Iran truce.

stories.title: Iran hangs man accused of ties to Israel and setting mosque on fire during pre-war protests
stories.summary: Iran executes Amirali Mirjafari for allegedly plotting to burn a Tehran mosque and collaborating with Israel and the US, as rights groups decry rising executions.

stories.title: Dozens of Turkish coal miners detained after march for unpaid wages
stories.summary: Some 110 Doruk Madencilik workers who had been marching to Ankara to demand unpaid wages and compensation were detained as demands for rights and safety grow louder.

stories.title: Gaza needs more than $71 billion for recovery and reconstruction, EU-UN report says
stories.summary: Gaza would need over $71 billion for recovery after two years of war, EU and UN warned Monday, as damage to homes, hospitals, and schools leaves most people displaced.

stories.title: How will Apple change under 'product guy' John Ternus?
stories.summary: How Ternus responds to challenges facing Apple will go a long way to deciding the tech giant's future, writes Zoe Kleinman.

stories.title: Humpback whale Timmy remains stranded in shallow waters in northern Germany
stories.summary: A stranded humpback whale in the Baltic Sea remains stuck near Poel island, Germany, as rescuers struggle to guide it back to deeper waters.

stories.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.summary: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.title: Zelensky says failure of US envoys to visit Kyiv is 'disrespectful'
stories.summary: US envoy Steve Witkoff and Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner have travelled to Moscow several times, but never to Kyiv.

stories.title: Mexico's president urges tighter security at tourist sites ahead of World Cup after shooting
stories.summary: Security officials said that the shooting at the world-famous Teotihuacan pyramids that left one person dead and 13 others wounded "wasn't spontaneous."

stories.title: Six stand trial in Istanbul over insecticide poisoning deaths of Turkish-German family
stories.summary: The Turkish-German Böcek family were staying at the Harbour Suites Old City hotel in Istanbul’s Fatih district when they fell ill on 12 November, the state-run Anadolu news agency said.

stories.title: From Epstein to sock puppets: Key takeaways from Kevin Warsh's Fed confirmation hearing
stories.summary: He denied making a deal with Donald Trump on interest rates and backed "policy regime change" at the central bank.

stories.title: Almost 8,000 people died or disappeared on migration routes in 2025, IOM says
stories.summary: The migration agency highlighted that at least around 340,000 family members are estimated to have been directly affected by the missing migrant crisis since 2014.

stories.title: UK ambassador to UAE warns of regional risk but backs UAE resilience amid fragile Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: In an interview with Euronews, British Ambassador to the United Arab Emirates Edward Hobart said the fragile US-Iran ceasefire should pave the way to a lasting peace with "a clear way forward".

stories.title: A Kashmir tourist hotspot became a bloodbath. A year on, the pain remains unbearable
stories.summary: A year after militants killed 26 people in Indian-administered Kashmir, families are trying to cope with their loss.

stories.title: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
stories.summary: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

stories.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.summary: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

stories.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
stories.summary: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

stories.title: Domestic workers legally recognised in Indonesia after '22-year struggle'
stories.summary: The country is home to some 4.2 million domestic workers - of which almost 90% are women.

stories.title: Iran seizes two cargo ships in Strait of Hormuz after three vessels fired on
stories.summary: The tanker seizures come after US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran was being indefinitely extended at the request of mediator Pakistan.
